events.description:

 Participants can choose a design from  Thingiverse , or provide their own design. Designs must be in an .stl file format and able to be emailed. We estimate that 3D prints will be ready for pickup within 7 days of submission. Designs are considered printable if able to load and save on a  Creality K1C , but the program lead has final say. events.description: Join us for a screening of How to Build a Library, a documentary about two Nairobi women who transformed a colonial-era library into a vibrant cultural hub.

 Free. No registration is required.

events.description:
Join us for socializing, finding new friends, networking, making jokes, learn to play Dungeons & Dragons, and having fun with art!  INSIDERS  programs are geared towards teens and adults with developmental and intellectual disabilities. Caregivers are welcome to join. Snacks will be provided.

events.title: Family Storytime
events.description:
Storytime is designed to accommodate a mix of ages and invites the entire family to participate in early literacy activities such as stories, songs, rhymes, fingerplays, and movement activities. Storytime is a fun and interactive experience for the whole family that promotes the joys of reading and learning for all ages. This program is geared for children of all ages and their parents/caregivers!  
  Free, with limited capacity.  
 Seating for Storytime is available on a first-come, first-serve

events.title: Family Storytime - Stay & Play
events.description:

Storytime is a fun and interactive experience for the whole family that promotes the joys of reading and learning for all ages. This program is geared for children of all ages and their parents/caregivers!     Storytime: 10:30am - 10:55am    Stay & Play: 11:00am - 11:30am

events.description:

  Free, 50 person maximum. Story Time tickets required. A second event, Infant and Toddler Storytime B will start at 11:15 for those that weren't able to make it inside for this event.

events.title: Black History Month Storytime
events.description:
Join us for a special storytime as we celebrate and learn about Black History Month through stories, songs, and more of your favorite storytime activities.  
 This program is in celebration of Black History Month.     
  Free, with limited capacity. Please get a ticket at the Information Desk up to 60 minutes prior to storytime.   This program is developed to align with  the Early Education Quality Standards .   ADA Accommodation Requests

events.title: My First Book Club
events.description:
My First Book Club was created to help caregivers and children bond over books and reading and to foster early literacy skills. Caregivers will learn the  theory and mechanics of a successful reading session while having fun sharing stories with their children and participating in various educational activities. Every participating child will receive a free book (while supplies last).    Suitable for children 6 months and older and their caregivers.  

  Free. Registration required.

events.description:
Please join us for Friends in Motion, a lively kids’ Zumba-inspired class featuring multicultural music, joyful movement, and a special Friendship Circle to celebrate connection and fun.  
 This program is in celebration of the Silicon Valley Reads 2026 theme “Bridges to Belonging" 
  
 This event is a part of the annual  Silicon Valley Reads (SV Reads)  program.

events.title: Thank You Neighbor: Thank You Cards
events.description:
In  Thank You, Neighbor  by Ruth Chan, we read about many ways neighbors can be helpful.  Think about someone in your neighborhood who helps.  What do they do?  How does that benefit the neighborhood?  Make them a thank you card to celebrate their efforts for the community.  You can write a message, draw a picture, or both!  
 This event is a part of the annual  Silicon Valley Reads (SV Reads)  program   ADA Accommodation Requests

events.title: Bilingual Reading to Children with Leila
events.description:
Participating in the Reading to Children program is a great way for children to develop their imagination and spark a love for books, reading and learning. Children will enjoy one-on-one or small group interaction with our volunteer. Kids are also welcome to practice reading to our volunteer.

This club is designed for children who are fans of the story and is conducted entirely in Mandarin Chinese. The event is organized by dedicated teen volunteers from Lynbrook High School.  小朋友们，你们听说过《 西游记 》吗？ 这是中国最有名的神话故事之一，讲述了一段惊险又有趣的旅程。在这个故事里，有聪明勇敢的 孙悟空 、善良慈悲的唐僧、憨厚搞笑的 猪八戒 ，还有忠诚可靠的沙和尚。他们一路上经历了许多危险和考验，但靠着智慧和团结，成功完成了任务。这次旅程不仅是他们追寻经书的故事，更是一场关于勇气、坚持和成长的冒险！更多有关   美猴王系列丛书 .

events.title: Homework Club at Edenvale Branch Library
events.description:
Need help with your homework? Kindergarten through 8th grade students can come to Edenvale Branch Library's Homework Club to get coached by trained library volunteers. Students can get free help with their homework in subjects such as reading, writing, math, and social studies!​  
 Homework Club at Edenvale Branch Library is scheduled for  Tuesdays and Thursdays each week from 4:00 PM – 6:00 PM , except for the following dates because of City holidays and school breaks:

events.description:

events.description:

This program creates a positive and supportive reading experience for beginning and developing readers, helping children build confidence and fluency.

events.description: Children in  grades K-3  are invited to j  oin us for the interactive program about bridges and their importance as routes connecting places, people, and communities. Children will learn about bridge types, famous bridges around the world, and why bridges stay strong and safe. They will get a chance to think like civil engineers with hands-on projects and a quiz!  This STEAM Club session is a part of the annual  Silicon Valley Reads (SV Reads)  program.  STEAM Club is  free  and all materia

events.title: Teens Reach
events.description:
Calling all teens between the ages of 13 and 18 looking 
 for simple volunteer opportunities.   
 Our youth department is building a seed library! That’s 
 right, a library for gardeners and future gardeners to be 
 able to check out seeds. And, we need you to help us 
 stuff and organize our seed packets. 
 Please email: eboni.harris@sjlibrary.org, if you are 
 interested in helping out on this day. 
    Free program. High and Middle Students welcome. No 
 registration.

events.description:
Drop in every Tuesday afternoon for a new STEAM adventure. 
   Jan 20 - Logic Games   Work independently or cooperatively on a variety of logic games.        
   Jan 27 - Fingerprinting (Science from Scientists)       Participants will discover which type of fingerprints they have before investigating the various ways of leaving behind, collecting, and analyzing prints. Students will explore fingerprint inking, dusting, lifting, and analysis.

events.description: Join us for a fun hour of reading aloud! The librarian will read a selection of picture books, easy readers and chapter books to entire families that foster engagement and social emotional learning.   According to research, reading aloud is one of the most important things we can do with children. Reading aloud builds many foundational skills, introduces vocabulary, provides a model of fluent, expressive reading, and helps children recognize what reading for pleasure is all about.  Free. No

events.description: Labors of Love delves into the remarkable life and enduring legacy of Henrietta Szold, one of the most influential, yet under-recognized, American Jewish women in history.

events.description:

They are also welcome to practice reading to our volunteer. Pre-registration is not required, and children may drop in to hear a story any time during the program. Join us as we celebrate another year of literacy and learning through intentional programming and storytelling during Read Across San José in March 2026. We’ll share stories, songs, and more of your Storytime favorites.

The program includes 20-25 minutes of stories and songs, followed by 40 minutes of open play with age appropriate toys.   All attendees will be required to observe the library's  Safety Guidelines

events.title: Thank You Neighbor: Thank You Cards
events.description: In  Thank You, Neighbor  by Ruth Chan, we read about many ways neighbors can be helpful.  Think about someone in your neighborhood who helps.  What do they do?  How does that benefit the neighborhood?  Make them a thank you card to celebrate their efforts for the community.  You can write a message, draw a picture, or both!   This event is a part of the annual  Silicon Valley Reads (SV Reads)  program   ADA Accommodation Requests

Using guided slides along with interactive, hands-on activities, participants will program a game where the player uses a sliding bucket to catch trash falling from the sky. Students will code using Scratch’s block-based programming language and learn fundamental concepts such as loops and conditional statements. Inspired by the Girls Who Code curriculum, this

events.title: Baby Lap-Sit Story Time
events.description:
This program is geared for pre-walking children and their parents or caregivers.   
 Baby Lap-Sit Storytime invites newborns, infants, and their caregivers to learn together by strengthening their relationship with each other through stories, songs, bounces, tickles, rhymes, books, and play. The program includes 20-25 minutes of stories and songs, followed by 40 minutes of open play with age appropriate toys.   All attendees will be required to observe the library's  Safety Guidelines

events.description:

In  Thank You, Neighbor  by Ruth Chan, we read about many ways neighbors can be helpful. Think about someone in your neighborhood who helps. What do they do? How does that benefit the neighborhood? Make them a thank you card to celebrate their efforts for the community. You can write a message, draw a picture, or both!  
 This event is a part of the annual  Silicon Valley Reads (SV Reads)  program.  Free. No registration required. All supplies provided. While supplies last.   ADA Accommodation R

events.title: 2nd Thursdays STEM Activity
events.description:
Come join us for a set of STEM activities presented by Lynbrook High School’s Women in STEM Club! Each month you'll explore a new set of topics as you learn about different fields of science and how they relate to the world. All materials will be provided.   Parental supervision is required.     A separate registration is required  for each monthly event  as space is limited.     This is a free program.

Storytime is a fun and interactive experience for the whole family that promotes the joys of reading and learning for all ages. This program is geared for children of all ages and their parents/caregivers!

events.title: One-On-One Tech Help
events.description:
Do you need help using a computer? Want to set up an email account? Stop by the Vineland Branch Library for a 30-minute session with our experienced volunteer! They can assist you with basic tech issues, such as:

events.description: Tune in to the Human Relations Commission Regular Meeting held in Council Chambers and virtually via Zoom

events.title: Finding Marian Abel at Los Altos  Library
events.description: Join longtime library commissioner Pierre Bedard and LAHS' Gustav Herhne for a special book club celebrating Marian Abel’s History of the Los Altos Library (1914-1964). The history, written in 1972, explores the library’s first 50 years. Register and get a PDF of her work so you can prepare for the book club. Marian Abel loved Los Altos. Let's celebrate our history and look forward to the next fifty years!    For accessibility accommodations (ex. ASL), please fill out the online form at sccld.or

events.description: Join us for our after-hours brain-game party for ages 21+!

No registration required but encouraged.   Register  here .

events.title: Silicon Valley Reads Book Discussion: "Give Me a Sign" by Anna Sortino
events.description: Join our Teens' Reach meeting to discuss the novel  "Give Me a Sign" by Anna Sortino .  For years, Lilah has felt suspended in a silent, lonely space -"stuck in the middle" between the vibrant hearing world and the rich, expressive Deaf one. Hard-of-hearing and tired of constantly navigating a world that wasn't built for her, she yearns for a place where she doesn't have to choose or apologize for who she is. That search for solid ground leads her to a life-changing summer where she wo

events.description: Join us for a hands-on, creative workshop where you’ll design and craft your very own sock baby. Along with the fun of making something unique, this experience explores meaningful themes in social science, emotional relationships, and the importance of caregiving. events.title: Storytime with AAC
events.description:
Enhance your child's communication with "Storytime with AAC"!   
 This program is designed for children with disabilities who use, or would benefit from, means of communication other than their voice. They use AAC devices, iPads, pictures, pointing, or exchanging pictures to communicate. It is designed for emergent communicators five years old and up (emergent communicators are new/existing AAC users who typically communicate with single words). Open to children of all abilities!

This club is designed for children who are fans of the story and is conducted entirely in Mandarin Chinese. The event is organized by dedicated teen volunteers from Lynbrook High School.  小朋友们，你们听说过《 西游记 》吗？ 这是中国最有名的神话故事之一，讲述了一段惊险又有趣的旅程。在这个故事里，有聪明勇敢的 孙悟空 、善良慈悲的唐僧、憨厚搞笑的 猪八戒 ，还有忠诚可靠的沙和尚。他们一路上经历了许多危险和考验，但靠着智慧和团结，成功完成了任务。这次旅程不仅是他们追寻经书的故事，更是一场关于勇气、坚持和成长的冒险！更多有关   美猴王系列丛书 .

events.title: Eurogames for Adults and Teens
events.location: West Valley — Group Study Room B
events.description: If you are an adult or teen who enjoys board games and strategy games, join us on the 3rd Wednesday of each month for Eurogames!   Eurogames are table-top games that emphasize strategic thinking and planning. Unlike many American board games, which often involve randomness and player elimination, Eurogames usually keep all players in the game and focus on resource management or building something (like cities, farms, or trade networks).   Free. No prior experience playing Eurogame

events.description:

This program is geared for children of all ages and their parents/caregivers!

Join us every Tuesday for an art activity! This week, in honor of Women's History Month, you can learn about the artist Georgia O'Keeffe and make flower art of your own!   
 Activities are directed to promote creative expression and de-stressing skills. Recommended for ages 5-12, but families are welcome. All supplies are provided. This program will take place in the Community Room. 
 March Events: 
   March 3 -  Alma Thomas and Geometric Art   March 10 -  Peg Doll Leprechauns    March 17 -  Mos

events.title: Homework Club
events.description:
Join us for Homework Club. We have supplies, space, and homework help.  When you complete your homework goal for the session you can pick a prize from the prize box. 
 Free. No registration required.

events.title: Homework Club at Edenvale Branch Library
events.description:
Need help with your homework? Kindergarten through 8th grade students can come to Edenvale Branch Library's Homework Club to get coached by trained library volunteers. Students can get free help with their homework in subjects such as reading, writing, math, and social studies!​  
 Homework Club at Edenvale Branch Library is scheduled for  Tuesdays and Thursdays each week from 4:00 PM – 6:00 PM , except for the following dates because of City holidays and school breaks:

events.title: LEGO® Fun
events.description:

events.location: King Library — Children's Exploration Room, Mezzanine
events.description:
Love balloons? Kids ages 6-11 will explore hands on balloon science through this STEM workshop that blends fizzy experiments, fun challenges while also practicing simple math skills. The fun ends with each student learning, creating and taking home a balloon animal.

events.location: Biblioteca Latinoamericana — Group Study Room A
events.description:
Reading Buddies provides students in grades K–5 with the opportunity to strengthen their reading skills by working one-on-one with a friendly reading coach. This program creates a positive and supportive reading experience for beginning and developing readers, helping children build confidence and fluency.

events.title: STEAM Club
events.description: Children in  grades K-3  are invited to join   us for  STEAM Club  every  Tuesday from 4:00-5:00 PM . Each week, participants will experience an exciting, new STEAM adventure involving hands-on activities that integrate different aspects of STEAM and showcase how STEAM concepts are applied in the real world (a list of session topics may be found  here ).  This program is free and all materials will be provided. Parents are welcome to attend with their children.&nbsp

events.description:

events.description:

events.title: SVR Together a Forest: Nature Craft
events.description: In  Together a Forest  by Roz MacLean, a class goes on a field trip to a forest, and each student looks for something in the forest to draw.  Let’s look for inspiration in nature and make our own art inspired by illustrations of forests in our picture books.   This event is a part of the annual  Silicon Valley Reads (SV Reads)  program. events.description:

Participants can follow ready-made templates or create their own designs. After building their designs on the pegboards, we will safely fuse the beads with heat so everyone can take home their finished design for themselves or others.

events.description: Canyon Conversations: Preserving Natural Darkness     Join McDonald Observatory and Grand Canyon Conservancy for a free, expert-led virtual panel discussion exploring the urgent need to preserve natural night skies.   Grand Canyon National Park enjoys some of the darkest night skies in the continental United States. events.title: (Virtual) Silicon Valley Reads Book Discussion: "Give Me a Sign" by Anna Sortino
events.description: Join us to discuss the novel  "Give Me a Sign" by Anna Sortino .  For years, Lilah has felt suspended in a silent, lonely space -"stuck in the middle" between the vibrant hearing world and the rich, expressive Deaf one. Hard-of-hearing and tired of constantly navigating a world that wasn't built for her, she yearns for a place where she doesn't have to choose or apologize for who she is. events.title: 星期一下午:西游记 (读书会)
events.description:
A book club dedicated to "Journey to the West," the classic Chinese fiction. This club is designed for children who are fans of the story and is conducted entirely in Mandarin Chinese. The event is organized by dedicated teen volunteers from Lynbrook High School.  小朋友们，你们听说过《 西游记 》吗？ 这是中国最有名的神话故事之一，讲述了一段惊险又有趣的旅程。在这个故事里，有聪明勇敢的 孙悟空 、善良慈悲的唐僧、憨厚搞笑的 猪八戒 ，还有忠诚可靠的沙和尚。他们一路上经历了许多危险和考验，但靠着智慧和团结，成功完成了任务。这次旅程不仅是他们追寻经书的故事，更是一场关于勇气、坚持和成长的冒险！更多有关   美猴王系列丛书 .

events.title: 星期一下午:西游记 (读书会)
events.description:
A book club dedicated to "Journey to the West," the classic Chinese fiction. This club is designed for children who are fans of the story and is conducted entirely in Mandarin Chinese. The event is organized by dedicated teen volunteers from Lynbrook High School.  小朋友们，你们听说过《 西游记 》吗？ 这是中国最有名的神话故事之一，讲述了一段惊险又有趣的旅程。在这个故事里，有聪明勇敢的 孙悟空 、善良慈悲的唐僧、憨厚搞笑的 猪八戒 ，还有忠诚可靠的沙和尚。他们一路上经历了许多危险和考验，但靠着智慧和团结，成功完成了任务。这次旅程不仅是他们追寻经书的故事，更是一场关于勇气、坚持和成长的冒险！更多有关   美猴王系列丛书 .

events.title: STEAM Sundays: Cactus Art & Science
events.description: Spring is upon us and soon summer will be here! California summers can be hot and dry, while some plants wither and die, others, like cactus and succulents thrive. Have you ever wondered why?  This month we'll focus on cactus, succulents, and other drought tolerant plants to learn more about their survival strategies. Want to grow in understanding basic math concepts and working on basic competitive math skills? Join us in Santa Teresa Junior Math Club and see your math and problem-solving skills improve! Concepts covered include rounding, solving with fractions, area, perimeter, data and graphs, money, polygons, angles, and decimals. 
 For children in 3rd through 5th grade (geared towards ages 8-11 years old). 
  Free. NEW - no registration required! Walk-ins welcome until program participant capacity reache

events.title: 星期一下午:西游记 (读书会)
events.description:
A book club dedicated to "Journey to the West," the classic Chinese fiction. This club is designed for children who are fans of the story and is conducted entirely in Mandarin Chinese. The event is organized by dedicated teen volunteers from Lynbrook High School.  小朋友们，你们听说过《 西游记 》吗？ 这是中国最有名的神话故事之一，讲述了一段惊险又有趣的旅程。在这个故事里，有聪明勇敢的 孙悟空 、善良慈悲的唐僧、憨厚搞笑的 猪八戒 ，还有忠诚可靠的沙和尚。他们一路上经历了许多危险和考验，但靠着智慧和团结，成功完成了任务。这次旅程不仅是他们追寻经书的故事，更是一场关于勇气、坚持和成长的冒险！更多有关   美猴王系列丛书 .   
  免费的亲子中文  活動.   座位数量有限.   门票

Please join us for Zumba for Kids, a high-energy program that combines music, movement, and fun in a playful group setting. Children will dance, jump, and follow simple routines that build coordination, confidence, and a love of physical activity. This program encourages self-expression while helping kids stay active and engaged.   

  Free. No Registration required.    ADA Accommodation Requests

events.description:

Proceeds go toward funding library programs, books, and supplies. Come support your local library branch and find new literary treasures for yourself.

events.title: Reading To Children
events.description: Participating in the Reading to Children program is a great way for children to develop their imagination and spark a love for books, reading and learning. events.title: 星期一下午:西游记 (读书会)
events.description:
A book club dedicated to "Journey to the West," the classic Chinese fiction. This club is designed for children who are fans of the story and is conducted entirely in Mandarin Chinese. The event is organized by dedicated teen volunteers from Lynbrook High School.  小朋友们，你们听说过《 西游记 》吗？ 这是中国最有名的神话故事之一，讲述了一段惊险又有趣的旅程。在这个故事里，有聪明勇敢的 孙悟空 、善良慈悲的唐僧、憨厚搞笑的 猪八戒 ，还有忠诚可靠的沙和尚。他们一路上经历了许多危险和考验，但靠着智慧和团结，成功完成了任务。这次旅程不仅是他们追寻经书的故事，更是一场关于勇气、坚持和成长的冒险！更多有关   美猴王系列丛书 .   
  免费的亲子中文  活動.   座位数量有限.   门票

events.title: Baby Lapsit Storytime at King
events.description:
This program is for babies that are  not yet walking confidently , and their caregivers.      
   Baby Lapsit invites newborns, infants, babies, and their caregivers to learn together and strengthen their relationship with each other through stories, songs, bounces, tickles, rhymes, books, and play. 
 After Storytime concludes, we invite you to Stay & Play! Some special toys and the Storytime librarian will join in the fun, giving babies and parents time to connect. 
   Lapsit Storytime 11:

events.title: Bilingual Family Storytime/ Hora de Cuentos & Stay and Play
events.description:
Bilingual Family storytime is geared for a mix of ages and invites the entire family to participate in early literacy activities such as stories, rhymes, fingerplays, and movement activities. After Storytime concludes, families are invited to Stay & Play! For 30 minutes after storytime, special, age-appropriate toys will be available to keep the fun going.  
   Storytime 11:00 - 11:30 am    Stay & Play 11:30 - 12:00 pm   
 Free. No registration is required. 
 Cuentos en familia es

events.title: My First Book Club
events.description:
My First Book Club was created to help caregivers and children bond over books and reading and to foster early literacy skills. Caregivers will learn the  theory and mechanics of a successful reading session while having fun sharing stories with their children and participating in various educational activities. Every participating child will receive a free book (while supplies last).    Suitable for children 6 months and older and their caregivers.  

  Free. Registration required.

events.title: Bilingual Reading to Children with Leila
events.description:
Participating in the Reading to Children program is a great way for children to develop their imagination and spark a love for books, reading and learning. Children will enjoy one-on-one or small group interaction with our volunteer. Kids are also welcome to practice reading to our volunteer.  
 Our volunteer, Ms. Leila, will read in either Spanish or English, or both.   
 Reading to Children will take place in the Children's Area. No tickets are necessary. 
  ADA Accommodation Request

events.title: Reading to Children
Let's Create
events.description:
Drop in  for an afternoon of art and crafts that is self-directed, choice-driven, and celebrates the experience of discovery.  This program is for children ages 5 and up.

 





 



 

This event is generously sponsored by the Friends of the Mountain View Library.

events.title: Junior STEAM Club
events.description:

events.title: Homework Club
events.description:
Join us for Homework Club. We have supplies, space, and homework help.  When you complete your homework goal for the session you can pick a prize from the prize box. 
 Free. No registration required. 
  
   ADA Accommodation Requests

events.title: Homework Coaching
events.location: King Library — Children's Room (Storytelling Area), 1st Floor
events.description:
This program is for students in Kindergarten through 6th grade that need support as they work on their homework.   Registration is preferred;   walk-ins accepted until 4:15pm if there are seats available.   
 Need help with your homework? Students in Kindergarten through 6th grade can meet with a Homework Coach in the Children's Room on Thursdays from 4pm - 5:30pm to get homework support.  Students must have homework to work on to participate in a session.  
  Free, with limited capacity.

events.title: Reading Buddies
events.description: Reading Buddies provides students in grades K-3 with an opportunity to improve their reading skills by working one-on-one with a volunteer. Reading Buddies creates a positive reading experience for beginning and developing readers and helps children build confidence as readers. Our volunteers will listen to children read and provide support in phonics, sight words, and comprehension. Students can bring their favorite book or choose from the available selection at the West Valley Branch

events.title: Reading To Children at the Willow Glen Library
events.description:
Participating in the Reading to Children program is a great way for children to develop their imagination and spark a love for books, reading and learning. Children will enjoy one-on-one or small group interaction with our volunteers Mike, Sally & Matt. Kids are also welcome to practice reading to our volunteer.  
    Free. No registration required.    ADA Accommodation Requests

events.title: Virtual Reality: Exploration & Experiences
events.description:
Join us every Thursday and experience the digital world like never before in our Virtual Reality program. Learn how to use our virtual reality equipment, try a virtual reality experience for the first time, and then come back again to try one of our more immersive experiences, games, and puzzles. 
 For teens ages 12 and up. 
 NOTE: The system may cause vertigo and disorientation. Please stop if you begin to feel any symptoms. 
  ADA Accommodation Requests

events.title: Family Storytime + Stay & Play
events.description:
Storytime is designed to accommodate a mix of ages and invites the entire family to participate in early literacy activities such as stories, songs, rhymes, fingerplays, and movement activities. Storytime is a fun and interactive experience for the whole family that promotes the joys of reading and learning for all ages. This program is geared for children of all ages and their parents/caregivers! 
 After Storytime concludes, families are invited to Stay & Play! For  30 minutes after storytime,

events.description:
This beginner course will introduce Sanskrit to children.  Students will begin by learning the alphabet. 
 For Ages 7-17. Free!

events.title: One-On-One Tech Help
events.description:
Do you need help using a computer? Want to set up an email account? Stop by the Vineland Branch Library for a 30-minute session with our experienced volunteer! They can assist you with basic tech issues, such as:  
  Setting up an email account, composing & sending emails, using email attachments   Web searches and basic data entry   Learning the basics of Microsoft Word and Excel and web-based productivity software such as Google docs   Using the library's pay-for-print and photocopy machines

events.title: Asistencia para Búsqueda de Trabajo

¿Necesitas ayuda para elaborar tu currículum? ¿Sabes dónde buscar oportunidades de empleo?   ¿Buscas un cambio de carrera?

  Crear tu resume y carta de presentación  Buscar oportunidades de empleo en sitios web  Llenar los formularios de solicitud de trabajo (verificación de historial, Formulario W-4, etc.).  Obtener información sobre técnicas de entrevista  Recibir apoyo en la inscripción del Curso de Mani

events.title: Itty Bitty Jamboree
events.description:
Adults and their babies (0 to 12 months old) join us for short, fun stories, rhymes, fingerplays, and songs. 
 Itty-Bitty Jamboree is 30 minutes followed by  Stay & Play time.   For accessibility accommodations (ex. ASL), please fill out the online form at sccld.org/accessibility 7+ days prior to the event. If you need assistance with the form, speak with a staff member at your local library, call (408) 540-3945, or email us at ask@lib.sccgov.org.

events.title: Storytime in Polish/Czytanie bajek po polsku
events.description: Come join us for a special Polish-language storytime! This family-friendly event features engaging stories read in Polish, opportunities to learn new words, and fun activities designed to spark imagination. Whether you’re a native speaker or just curious about the language, everyone is welcome to take part in this warm and joyful experience.  Offered monthly, this engaging program is primarily for youth ages 3–7 and their families, but all ages and language abilities are welcome to join!   Zapra

events.title: Knit-Alongs at Cupertino Library
events.description:
Starting Time: 2:30 pm Ending Time: 4:00 pm   Community Room 201A   On the first Friday of each month we will meet in Room 201B  
      Cupertino Library Knit Alongs continue in 2026!.  All adult knitters and crocheters are welcome. No registration or sign up required.  We're always happy to meet someone new, so please feel free to join us.  Meet new friends, start a new project, or finish an old one.    *Please note, This is mostly a social group for

Tabletop/windows/grounds cleanup – Help reinvigorate our branch by wiping down tabletops, cleaning dusty windows, and picking up litter outside the library.

events.description: The Cricut Open Lab is a 45-minute learning session on select Fridays with machines available to use. First-time users can learn what the Cricut machine is and how it works. You can also watch  these tutorial videos  ahead of time to get a better idea of how to use the Cricut machine and Design Space. For those who already have experience using Cricut, they are welcome to register to use a machine independently to cut out their designs. We will have Cricut machines (Explore/Air and Mak

events.title: Friday Fun
events.description:
Each week will feature a special craft or activity, so you can get creative. Come together to have fun and build community! 
 Children, teens, and adults, and families are invited to play a selection of board games and card games that are available.   Free. No registration required.   
  ADA Accommodation Requests

events.title: Illuminated Crafts: Sensory Light Lightsaber
events.description:
Learn the science of various forms of light emissions. From bioluminescence to phosphorescence, we will explore how these different forms of light are produced, and reproduce one form of light by creating a glow in the dark lightsaber! 
 This STEAM program is recommended for ages 8 and above. 
 Registration is required. 
  ADA Accommodation Requests

events.title: LEGO® Engineering Club
STEM Fridays: Linkages
events.description: Linkages is an exciting exploration of mechanical motion and a favorite of many museums and makerspaces. A linkage is created by connecting two or more rigid parts to make a flexible hinge.   You can keep it simple, or develop complex mechanical motion by connecting multiple linkages to create all kinds of contraptions.   Join us for STEM Fridays where we have different experiments and topics every week! Learn about the science behind lava lamps, slime, bubbles, potions, and more! Develop coding

events.title: Teens Reach at Seven Trees Library
events.description:
Teens Reach is the youth council for the San José Public Library for ages 13 to 18, providing a key way for young adults to build their leadership skills by developing and participating in library activities, as well as becoming library advisors and advocates. 
 Seven Tree's Teens Reach group meets monthly on the last Friday with socializing and fun from 4 to 5 p.m.       
   February 27th:  In Person   March 27th : Virtual   April 24th : In Person   May 29th : Virtual   June-July : Summer Break

events.title: 星期一下午:西游记 (读书会)
events.description:
A book club dedicated to "Journey to the West," the classic Chinese fiction. This club is designed for children who are fans of the story and is conducted entirely in Mandarin Chinese. The event is organized by dedicated teen volunteers from Lynbrook High School.  小朋友们，你们听说过《 西游记 》吗？ 这是中国最有名的神话故事之一，讲述了一段惊险又有趣的旅程。在这个故事里，有聪明勇敢的 孙悟空 、善良慈悲的唐僧、憨厚搞笑的 猪八戒 ，还有忠诚可靠的沙和尚。他们一路上经历了许多危险和考验，但靠着智慧和团结，成功完成了任务。这次旅程不仅是他们追寻经书的故事，更是一场关于勇气、坚持和成长的冒险！更多有关   美猴王系列丛书 .   
  免费的亲子中文  活動.   座位数量有限.   门票
